Why should I give you my ID card
item.description: Today, Youtube asked me to verify my age. To do so, I got asked to either enter a credit card in my name, or to upload my ID card. And that’s a no. I won’t. Why should I? Youtube and European regulations Digging more into this, I find out that Youtube posted a blog on September 22, 2020, annoucing the new changes to their policy. In this blog post, titled Using technology to more consistently apply age restrictions, Youtube team goes over the forthcoming European Union’s Audiovisual Media Services Directive, and how, under this directive, they have to make sure their user is over 18 to access restricted content.

item.title: Systems design explains the world: volume 1 - apenwarr
supercookie • welcome
Damned if you do, damned if you don’t: Censorship, Decentralized Networks and Moderation | Hacker Noon
Into the world of digital identities
item.description: Into the world of digital identities The move to work from home (and not work remote) came with a lot of challenge. A growing pain for me has been the lack of a printer for administrative red tapes which is still a very paper based process. Digital identity platform such as France Connect or Gov Gateway in the UK (swissID, spain/estonia smart cartd system, …) should make this easier.

item.title: Chromium support for the Distributed Web
item.description: Chromium safelist dweb URL schemes Today, as part of a change by Frédéric Wang, Chromium allows website to register their ability to support distributed web URL schemes. Website gateways, such as Cloudflare IPFS Gateway or IPFS.io would be able to use Chrome navigator API to serve Native DWeb URLs. These new schemes are the following: Organisation scheme Example URL Cabal Club cabal cabal://1eef9ad64e284691b7c6f6310e39204b5f92765e36102046caaa6a7ff8c02d74 Dat Foundation dat dat://778f8d955175c92e4ced5e4f5563f69bfec0c86cc6f670352c457943666fe639/dat_intro.
